title: Record Schemas, Field Provenance, Confidence, and Data Quality · Narrowing Workshop
summary: A disability-led public-interest technology team incrementally builds loss-aware versioned site record from original deterministic HTTP, robots, sitemap, HTML, browser, and report fixtures while retaining prior TypeScript, Node, web, Git, accessibility, security, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of loss-aware versioned site record while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case…
objectives: Version admitted records, field meanings, missingness, constraints, and compatible migrations independently from source markup.
Attach source URL, response identity, node or attribute path, raw value digest, transform version, time, and decision to each field.
Use confidence only when tied to observable rule evidence, calibration, thresholds, and review consequences.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Record Schema Version
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 9093 during an isolated browser, package, and restore smoke. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile versioned records, field provenance, confidence evidence, quality counts, and corrections; URL, robots, body, parser, frontier, privacy, accessibility, and report budgets stay explicit; then inject one authenticated, private-data, output-injection, path, log, plugin, or correction fault and contain it. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Record Schema Version.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for version admitted records, field meanings, missingness, constraints, and compatible migrations independently from source markup. For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Record Schema Version, keep Record Schema Version connected to executable TypeScript authorization, URL, runtime admission, Fetch, Promise, browser, accessibility, report, package, and recovery decision evidence.
